rejected_candidates.text: Familial Dysbetalipoproteinemia (Type III Hyperlipoproteinemia) severe hypertriglyceridemia and hypercholesterolemia (both often > 300), associated with premature diffuse vascular disease, male predominance, Palmar xanthomas are pathognomonic
rejected_candidates.reason: The text does not explicitly support a relationship between Familial Dysbetalipoproteinemia and hyperlipidemia. It only mentions that familial dysbetalipoproteinemia is associated with hypertriglyceridemia and hypercholesterolemia.

rejected_candidates.text: Hepatic Lipase Deficiency Rare disorder with very high cholesterol and triglyceride concentrations, phenotypically similar to familial dysbetalipoproteinemia.
rejected_candidates.reason: The text does not support a relationship between Hepatic Lipase Deficiency and hyperlipidemia. It only mentions that it is associated with familial dysbetalipoproteinemia.

rejected_candidates.text: LPL deficiency Results in elevated chylomicrons, which carry dietary fat; chylomicrons are generally not present after an overnight fast, so a creamy looking plasma in a fasting specimen should be a clue to the diagnosis, especially if seen in young children; extremely high triglycerides can lead to pancreatitis
rejected_candidates.reason: The text does not support a relationship between LPL deficiency and hyperlipidemia. It only mentions that it is associated with elevated chylomicrons.

rejected_candidates.text: ApoCII deficiency This apolipoprotein is an activator of LPL; its absence causes a clinical picture identical to LPL deficiency
rejected_candidates.reason: The text does not support a relationship between ApoCII deficiency and hyperlipidemia. It only mentions that it is associated with LPL deficiency.

rejected_candidates.text: Familial hypertriglyceridemia Autosomal dominant inheritance; Main defect is overproduction of VLDL triglycerides by the liver
rejected_candidates.reason: The text does not support a relationship between Familial hypertriglyceridemia and hyperlipidemia. It only mentions that it is associated with overproduction of VLDL triglycerides.
